ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE SET FOR NOV-22-2004 IN DEPARTMENT 212 AT 1:30 PM FOR FAILURE TO OBTAIN AN ANSWER(S) FROM, OR ENTER DEFAULT(S) AGAINST, DEFENDANT(S) LISA LOCATI, AND MICHAEL LOCATI. THE OCT-08-2004 CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

DISCOVERY 610, NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: THE COURT HAS REVIEWED THE LETTER OF SEPTEMBER 13, 2004 SUBMITTED BY COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH IS GRANTED. A SUBPOENA DUCES TECUM IS AN UNAUTHORIZED METHOD OF DISCOVERY OF CONFIDENTIAL PEACE OFFICER PERSONNEL RECORDS AS DEFINED IN PENAL CODE SECTION 832.8. A PERSON APPOINTED OR DEPUTIZED AS A RESERVE OR AUXILIARY CITY POLICE OFFICER PURSUANT TO PENAL CODE SECTION 830.6 AND ASSIGNED SPECIFIC POLICE FUNCTIONS IS DEEMED A PEACE OFFICER IF HE OR SHE HAS FULFILLED SPECIFIED TRAINING REQUIREMENTS. ACCORDINGLY, DISCOVERY OF PLAINTIFF'S PEACE OFFICER PERSONNEL RECORDS IS SUBJECT TO PENAL CODE SECTION 832.7 THAT PROVIDES IN PERTINENT PART, "PEACE OFFICER . . . PERSONNEL RECORDS . . . ARE CONFIDENTIAL AND SHALL NOT BE DISCLOSED IN ANY CRIMINAL OR CIVIL PROCEEDING EXCEPT BY DISCOVERY PURSUANT TO SECTIONS 1043 AND 1046 OF THE EVIDENCE CODE." (610/CHAN)

Further Hearing On Defendant'S Motion To Compel -- In Camera Review
Off Calendar May-26-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jaime M. Gher, Esq. of HANSON BRIDGETT 995-5049, appearing for defendant/moving party Golden Gate Bridge
No appearance for plaintiff.

RULING:
Defendant's motion for discovery of peace officer personnel records as to the East Palo Alto Police Department and the Grant Unified School District -- The Court has conducted an in camera review of personnel records from the East Palo Alto Police Department and will produce copies of specified documents subject to the parties executing a stipulated protective order that counsel for defendant may not disclose or permit to be disclosed the records produced pursuant to the motion for purposes unrelated to the litigation. Counsel for defendant may disclose or permit to be disclosed the records to persons employed by counsel to assist in the preparation of the case, if that disclosure is required for that preparation. Persons provided this information by counsel shall be informed that further dissemination of the information constitutes a violation of a court order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

RULING:
Argued and TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED WITH MODIFICATIONS: Defendant's motion to compel the attendance of plaintiff at deposition is granted. No opposition has been filed. Plaintiff is ordered to appear for deposition on May 27, 2005 at 9:30 a.m. at 333 Market Street, Suite 2100, San Francisco, California. Failure to appear on two previous occasions for deposition was a misuse of the discovery process. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1036.30 are stayed pending compliance with court orders, said stay to become permanent if plaintiff complies. Order signed in open court. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E.
